deck: Chesca Colloredo-Mansfeld left the corporate world to co-found MiracleFeet and bet that one simple, low-cost treatment for clubfoot could reach every child born with it. A story about following your instincts and backing them with data.
seo_description: How Chesca Colloredo-Mansfeld built MiracleFeet from one idea into a global organization treating clubfoot, on track to reach 100,000 children.
og_description: Chesca Colloredo-Mansfeld on building MiracleFeet to make clubfoot treatment reach every child born with it.
about: One in 800 babies are born with clubfoot, a treatable impairment that, left untreated, prevents a child from walking and becomes a root cause of illiteracy, malnutrition, and poverty in many parts of the world. The condition is routinely treated at birth in high income countries, but children born in low and middle income countries have historically lacked access to quality care.

Chesca Colloredo-Mansfeld co-founded MiracleFeet in 2010, driven by the idea that a newly accepted, low-cost, non-surgical treatment, the Ponseti method, could have an outsized impact. Co-hosts Jonathan Jackson and Amie Vaccaro talk with her about leaving the corporate world to become a social entrepreneur, raising money for a problem no one had heard of, and the role data and CommCare played in proving impact and earning a GiveWell grant.

Today MiracleFeet is the largest global organization solely dedicated to ending clubfoot as a solvable problem. The team is on track to serve 100,000 children, with a goal of treating 70% of clubfoot cases in the countries where they work.

pull_quote.text: This is so fixable. There aren't many problems like that, where you're kind of like, this is a no brainer. We have to do this.

bullets: From corporate to clubfoot: the moment a dying doctor's research convinced Chesca to leave business and solve one pressing problem
Following your gut, backed by data: trusting instinct, then proving it with research, evidence, and ROI
The hardest sell in fundraising: raising money for a condition most donors had never heard of, starting with friends and family
Earning a GiveWell grant: 13 years of persistence, Cochrane evidence, and the data CommCare helped collect
Data for the front line, not just funders: building a tool that made clinic visits easier and put data in local partners' hands
An exit strategy that sticks: training medical schools and ministries so clubfoot care outlives MiracleFeet
Letting go of what you built: a founder transition, a new CEO, and life after handing over the reins

guests.name: Chesca Colloredo-Mansfeld
guests.role: Co-founder and former CEO who built the largest global organization dedicated to ending clubfoot, now serving on its board and mentoring early-stage founders.
